summary: Benn Stancil argues that self-serve BI keeps failing because teams define it as a code-free version of analyst work, which is the wrong goal. Analysts create new measures to test hypotheses, while non-analysts mostly want to extract known metrics, so self-serve should be built around opinionated “metric extraction” rather than open-ended analysis.
money_quotes: Self-serve initiatives try to solve the wrong problem.
Analysts and non-analysts use data in structurally different ways.
Non-analysts work like journalists, collating existing metrics and drawing conclusions by considering them in their totality.
The more questions people can theoretically self-serve, the fewer they can practically self-serve.
Opinionated simplicity is better than indifferent optionality.
key_insight: Self-serve BI should focus on constrained metric extraction, not on replicating analysts’ open-ended analytical work.
